thought.text: I will create the `services.yaml` blueprint file. This defines the `Service` model, its columns, relationships, and role-based permissions. Note that `staff` can create and update services but cannot update the `status` field or destroy services. Customers can only read services.

action.file_text:
model: Service
slug: services
table: services

options:
  belongs_to_organization: true
  soft_deletes: false
  audit_trail: false
  pagination: true
  per_page: 25

columns:
  name:
    type: string
    nullable: false
    searchable: true
  description:
    type: text
    nullable: true
    searchable: true
  duration_minutes:
    type: integer
    nullable: true
  price:
    type: decimal
    nullable: true
    precision: 10
    scale: 2
  status:
    type: string
    default: "draft"
    nullable: false
    filterable: true

relationships:
  - type: belongsTo
    model: Organization
  - type: hasMany
    model: TimeSlot

permissions:
  owner:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  admin:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  staff:
    actions: [index, show, store, update]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields:
      - name
      - description
      - duration_minutes
      - price
  customer:
    actions: [index, show]
    show_fields:
      - id
      - name
      - description
      - duration_minutes
      - price
      - status
    create_fields: []
    update_fields: []

tool_call.name: guarded_file_editor
tool_call.arguments:
